name: Towards the Chapelle St Roch de Monlaur
name_original: Vers la Chapelle St Roch de Monlaur
region_intro: Touring cycling around Chélan features a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural areas, providing varied terrain for cyclists. The region includes routes that traverse open countryside and pass by notable features such as Lac de la Gimone. Cyclists can expect a mix of moderate climbs and flatter sections, suitable for different fitness levels. The area's natural setting offers a tranquil backdrop for long-distance rides. Best touring cycling routes around Chélan The most popular touring cycling route is Lac de la Gimone and Sansan, a 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route offers a substantial challenge for experienced touring cyclists. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Towards the Chapelle St Roch de Monlaur, a moderate 14.3 miles (23.0 km) path. This route leads towards a historic chapel, providing scenic views along the way. Local touring cyclists also love the Château de Saint-Blancard – Lac de la Gimone loop from Lunax, a 13.3 miles (21.3 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and past Lac de la Gimone, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Chélan is defined by rolling hills, open countryside, and routes passing by lakes and châteaux. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Chélan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 92 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Chélan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Chélan?
A: There are nearly 50 touring cycling routes available in Chélan, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Chélan?
A: The routes in Chélan cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 5 easy routes, 27 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Chélan?
A: Yes, for those seeking a substantial challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021123199/lac-de-la-gimone-and-sansan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de la Gimone and Sansan&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 56.9-mile (91.5 km) trail that takes approximately 6 hours to complete.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Chélan?
A: Touring cycling in Chélan is characterized by rolling hills, open countryside, and agricultural areas. Many routes pass by picturesque lakes like Lac de la Gimone and Lac de l'Astarac, as well as historic châteaux.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Chélan?
A: Yes, Chélan offers several loop routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26032844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Saint-Blancard – Lac de la Gimone loop from Lunax&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.3-mile (21.3 km) trail that takes you through agricultural landscapes and past Lac de la Gimone.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Chélan?
A: The touring cycling routes in Chélan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the tranquil backdrop of the natural setting, and the scenic views along routes that pass by lakes and châteaux.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see along the touring cycling routes in Chélan?
A: Along the routes, you can explore several interesting points. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4814164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Saint-Blancard&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orientation table&lt;/a&gt;, or see the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Astarac&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2011316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boulogne-sur-Gesse Church Square&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernet Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also notable attractions.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930585876/towards-the-chapelle-st-roch-de-monlaur" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towards the Chapelle St Roch de Monlaur&lt;/a&gt; route is a 14.3-mile (23.0 km) path that leads towards a historic chapel, offering scenic views along the way.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes in Chélan?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate the region's beautiful lakes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20527705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Saint-Blancard – Lac de la Gimone loop from Thermes-Magnoac&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.7-mile (42.9 km) route that features Lac de la Gimone.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that follow rivers in Chélan?
A: Yes, you can find routes that trace the local waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35720960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the Gers loop from Cizos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.2-mile (42.2 km) route that allows you to cycle alongside the Gers river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Chélan?
A: While specific seasonal details are not provided, the region's rolling hills and agricultural landscapes suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery for touring cycling, avoiding the potential heat of summer or chill of winter.
